primary_question.title: Design a Video Streaming Platform
primary_question.context: Design a video streaming service similar to YouTube. Users can upload videos, which are then processed and made available for streaming globally. The platform serves 2 billion logged-in users per month, with 500 hours of video uploaded every minute.
scope_hints: Upload flow vs. playback flow — which to focus on?
Live streaming or recorded video only?
Geographic scope — global or specific regions?

faang_bar.ic: Staff/Senior IC should drive the problem definition themselves, propose a coherent architecture covering upload, processing, and delivery, go deep on at least 2 areas (usually encoding pipeline + CDN), discuss failure modes, back-of-envelope estimates without prompting, and make opinionated tradeoff decisions with clear reasoning.
faang_bar.sdm: SDM should show system thinking at the organizational level — how teams would own different components, what the build vs buy decisions are, how they'd phase the rollout, what the operational challenges are, and how they'd balance technical debt vs. velocity. Less expected to go deep on encoding specifics, more expected on organizational and delivery tradeoffs.

sections.name: Requirements Clarification
objectives: Candidate asks clarifying questions before diving in
good_answer_signals: Asks about scale before designing anything
weak_answer_signals: Jumps straight to architecture without clarifying
Only asks one or two surface questions
probe_questions: What's your assumption on the read-to-write ratio?
How are you thinking about consistency requirements here?
Walk me through your back-of-the-envelope on storage needs.

sections.name: High Level Architecture
objectives: Major services separated with clear responsibilities
good_answer_signals: Makes opinionated database choices with reasoning
Identifies the hardest parts of the system proactively
probe_questions: Why did you choose that database over alternatives?
Walk me through what happens when a user uploads a video — end to end.
Where are the bottlenecks in this design?
How does your CDN strategy work here?

sections.name: Deep Dive — Core Component
objectives: At least one component explored in significant depth
Scaling strategy for the hardest component
good_answer_signals: Goes beyond happy path to failure scenarios
Discusses specific algorithms or data structures
weak_answer_signals: Stays at high level without going deeper
probe_questions: What happens if the encoding worker crashes mid-job?
How do you handle a sudden 10x spike in uploads?
Walk me through your video chunking strategy.
How does your system handle a CDN node going down?

sections.name: Tradeoffs & Alternatives
objectives: Alternative approaches considered and rejected with reasoning
Awareness of what the design optimizes for and what it sacrifices
good_answer_signals: Proactively mentions what their design gives up
Shows awareness of alternative architectural patterns
Comfortable saying 'this is the wrong call if X changes'
weak_answer_signals: Defends every decision without acknowledging downsides
Can't articulate what the design is optimized for
probe_questions: What's the biggest risk in this design?
What would you do differently if consistency mattered more than availability?
If you had to cut this down to an MVP, what goes first?

sections.name: Reliability & Operations
weak_answer_signals: No mention of how they'd know if something breaks
probe_questions: How would you know if the encoding pipeline was degraded?
What does your rollback strategy look like?
Walk me through your on-call runbook for a P0 here.
